# HealthService: surface effective unified/federated topology in /health

## Context

Surfaced during #10001 self-review (PR #10121) and re-flagged on #10007 self-review (PR #10123). Now that the unified-topology pillar of sub-epic #10015 is complete (#10001 routing + #10007 lifecycle bypass, both merged), operators deploying Memory Core in unified mode need a way to verify the flag resolved as expected without inspecting logs or re-running the config through `node -e`.

## The Problem

Today's `HealthService.healthcheck()` returns `{engines: {chroma: true}}` — a binary reachability flag. It does not expose:

- The effective topology (`unified` / `federated`)
- The effective ChromaDB coordinates the client is targeting (`{host, port}`)
- Which config path won the resolution (`engines.chroma` vs `engines.kb.chroma`)

Operators running `curl .../healthcheck` in a cloud container have no in-band way to confirm they set `NEO_CHROMA_UNIFIED=true` correctly and that the client is pointed at the KB's instance. A misconfiguration where `chromaUnified` is forgotten would be silent: MC would spin up its own ChromaDB (federated path), mount a distinct volume, and populate a distinct collection set — diverging from the KB's state without any operator-visible signal until cross-tenant drift emerges.

## The Architectural Reality

The information to surface already lives in-process, thanks to #10001 and #10007:

- `aiConfig.chromaUnified` — the flag itself
- `ChromaManager.resolveChromaCoordinates(aiConfig)` — returns the effective `{host, port}` per topology (extracted as a pure method in #10001 specifically to make this kind of aggregation possible without singleton re-instantiation)
- `ai/mcp/server/memory-core/services/HealthService.mjs` — the existing healthcheck implementation

The fix is pure aggregation — no new resolution logic, no config duplication.

**Proposed response shape:**

```json
{
  "status": "healthy",
  "database": {
    "connection": {"connected": true, "collections": {...}},
    "topology": {
      "mode": "unified",
      "coordinates": {"host": "localhost", "port": 8000},
      "resolvedVia": "engines.kb.chroma"
    }
  }
}
```

`resolvedVia` names the config key path that won the resolution — operator gets a direct pointer to what to check if coordinates look wrong.

## The Fix

1. Extend `HealthService.healthcheck()` to call `ChromaManager.resolveChromaCoordinates(aiConfig)` and include the resolution under `database.topology`.
2. Add a Playwright spec at `test/playwright/unit/ai/mcp/server/memory-core/services/HealthService.spec.mjs` (new file) asserting:
   - `topology.mode === 'federated'` when `chromaUnified=false`
   - `topology.mode === 'unified'` when `chromaUnified=true`
   - `coordinates` matches `aiConfig.engines.chroma` / `aiConfig.engines.kb.chroma` respectively
   - `resolvedVia` names the correct config key path
3. Update `learn/agentos/MemoryCore.md` to document the new `/health` topology fields (can absorb the deferred doc pass from #10001 + #10007).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `healthcheck` MCP tool response includes `database.topology` with `{mode, coordinates, resolvedVia}` fields.
- [ ] Federated-mode response shows `mode: 'federated'`, coordinates matching `engines.chroma`, `resolvedVia: 'engines.chroma'`.
- [ ] Unified-mode response shows `mode: 'unified'`, coordinates matching `engines.kb.chroma`, `resolvedVia: 'engines.kb.chroma'`.
- [ ] New `HealthService.spec.mjs` covers both topology branches.
- [ ] `learn/agentos/MemoryCore.md` documents the new fields.

## Out of Scope

- Changing the flag resolution logic itself — only the reporting surface. `ChromaManager.resolveChromaCoordinates` is the single source of truth and stays unchanged.
- Adding topology reporting for non-ChromaDB subsystems (SQLite graph path, inference provider, embedding provider). Those are separate follow-up tickets if the pattern proves valuable.

## Related

- Closes the follow-up commitment from PR #10121 (#10001) and PR #10123 (#10007)
- Consumes `ChromaManager.resolveChromaCoordinates(aiConfig)` extracted in #10001
- Unblocks operator-facing diagnostics for sub-epic #10015 deployments

- 2026-05-04T20:59:46Z @neo-opus-ada referenced in commit `207435e` - "docs(agentos): clarify per-server healthcheck shape + smoke-test surfaces (#10694)

Addresses Cycle 1 review (PRR_kwDODSospM77vMjD) on PR #10716. Two doc-contract
corrections in the Migration section:

- Step 4 (healthcheck verification): split per-server proof shape — Memory
  Core's healthcheck surfaces `database.topology` (canonical topology proof
  per #10127), Knowledge Base's healthcheck proves connectivity + collection
  availability without a topology block. Cross-server consistency is the
  end-to-end verification primitive.
- Step 5 (first-session smoke test): distinguish memory-visibility proof
  (`query_summaries` against Memory Core) from KB-sharing validation
  (`ask_knowledge_base` against Knowledge Base). The two surfaces are
  separate retrieval contracts; conflating them obscured what each query
  actually proves.

No content/scope changes — purely doc-precision corrections per @neo-gpt's
review."
- 2026-05-04T21:24:11Z @tobiu referenced in commit `e2210a8` - "docs(agentos): document shared KB/MC team deployment profile (#10694) (#10716)

* docs(agentos): document shared KB/MC team deployment profile (#10694)

Adds learn/agentos/SharedDeployment.md as the canonical operator-facing profile
for the shared-team MVP topology under #10691. Indexes the new doc in
learn/tree.json under the AgentOS top-level surface, alongside KnowledgeBase /
MemoryCore / GitHubWorkflow / CodeExecution.

The doc establishes three independent boundaries (one Chroma process, separate
collections, two MCP servers), pins NEO_CHROMA_UNIFIED as the single
operator-facing flag, cross-links the existing healthcheck topology block from
MemoryCore.md (#10127), and documents migration from per-developer local mode.

#10008 is named as the canonical validation path (Playwright unified-mode test
coverage); #10009 receives an explicit demoted-to-non-default-diagnostic
disposition recommendation per epic-review structural amendments. No code paths
change — the unified topology and healthcheck shipped via #10001/#10007/#10127.

* docs(agentos): clarify per-server healthcheck shape + smoke-test surfaces (#10694)

Addresses Cycle 1 review (PRR_kwDODSospM77vMjD) on PR #10716. Two doc-contract
corrections in the Migration section:

- Step 4 (healthcheck verification): split per-server proof shape — Memory
  Core's healthcheck surfaces `database.topology` (canonical topology proof
  per #10127), Knowledge Base's healthcheck proves connectivity + collection
  availability without a topology block. Cross-server consistency is the
  end-to-end verification primitive.
- Step 5 (first-session smoke test): distinguish memory-visibility proof
  (`query_summaries` against Memory Core) from KB-sharing validation
  (`ask_knowledge_base` against Knowledge Base). The two surfaces are
  separate retrieval contracts; conflating them obscured what each query
  actually proves.

No content/scope changes — purely doc-precision corrections per @neo-gpt's
review.

- 2026-05-05T18:43:19Z @neo-opus-ada cross-referenced by #10773
- 2026-05-05T18:55:38Z @neo-opus-ada referenced in commit `fb1d33d` - "feat(memory-core): surface active embedding provider in healthcheck (#10723)

Adds buildEmbeddingProviderBlock pure projection to HealthService.mjs, wired
into the healthcheck payload as a new top-level providers.embedding field.
Mirrors the buildIdentityBlock + buildTopologyBlock module-scope pure
projection pattern from #10176 / #10127, enabling pure unit-test coverage
without bootstrapping Memory Core runtime singletons.

Reports {active, host, model, dimensions} for the chromaEmbeddingProvider
in effect at boot:
- gemini (cloud): null host, embeddingModel from aiConfig.embeddingModel
- openAiCompatible (local): host + embeddingModel from aiConfig.openAiCompatible
- ollama (local): host + embeddingModel from aiConfig.ollama
- unrecognized: surfaces error field naming the misconfig directly

Operator config path documented in learn/agentos/SharedDeployment.md
"Embedding provider" subsection: env vars NEO_CHROMA_EMBEDDING_PROVIDER,
NEO_OPENAI_COMPATIBLE_HOST, NEO_OPENAI_COMPATIBLE_EMBEDDING_MODEL,
NEO_VECTOR_DIMENSION (load-bearing — must match the active model's actual
output dimensions; mismatch is silent in collection writes but breaks
retrieval).

Substrate observation flagged in SharedDeployment.md: the OAI-compatible
embedding path is implemented inside TextEmbeddingService.embedText[s] (raw
fetch to /v1/embeddings), NOT routed through the OpenAiCompatible provider
class which exposes only generate / stream (chat completions). Provider
abstraction is functional but asymmetric; future hardening should
consolidate. Out of scope for #10723.

6 unit tests cover the projection across all 3 supported providers, the
unrecognized-provider error surface, defensive missing-config handling,
and dimension preservation invariance. All pass at HEAD."
- 2026-05-05T19:00:23Z @tobiu referenced in commit `24fa125` - "feat(memory-core): surface active embedding provider in healthcheck (#10723) (#10767)

Adds buildEmbeddingProviderBlock pure projection to HealthService.mjs, wired
into the healthcheck payload as a new top-level providers.embedding field.
Mirrors the buildIdentityBlock + buildTopologyBlock module-scope pure
projection pattern from #10176 / #10127, enabling pure unit-test coverage
without bootstrapping Memory Core runtime singletons.

Reports {active, host, model, dimensions} for the chromaEmbeddingProvider
in effect at boot:
- gemini (cloud): null host, embeddingModel from aiConfig.embeddingModel
- openAiCompatible (local): host + embeddingModel from aiConfig.openAiCompatible
- ollama (local): host + embeddingModel from aiConfig.ollama
- unrecognized: surfaces error field naming the misconfig directly

Operator config path documented in learn/agentos/SharedDeployment.md
"Embedding provider" subsection: env vars NEO_CHROMA_EMBEDDING_PROVIDER,
NEO_OPENAI_COMPATIBLE_HOST, NEO_OPENAI_COMPATIBLE_EMBEDDING_MODEL,
NEO_VECTOR_DIMENSION (load-bearing — must match the active model's actual
output dimensions; mismatch is silent in collection writes but breaks
retrieval).

Substrate observation flagged in SharedDeployment.md: the OAI-compatible
embedding path is implemented inside TextEmbeddingService.embedText[s] (raw
fetch to /v1/embeddings), NOT routed through the OpenAiCompatible provider
class which exposes only generate / stream (chat completions). Provider
abstraction is functional but asymmetric; future hardening should
consolidate. Out of scope for #10723.

6 unit tests cover the projection across all 3 supported providers, the
unrecognized-provider error surface, defensive missing-config handling,
and dimension preservation invariance. All pass at HEAD.

Co-authored-by: tobiu <tobiasuhlig78@gmail.com>"
